---
title: "Economic Snapshot"
---

{{< include _setup.qmd >}}

```{r}
#| results: asis
if (USING_SAMPLE_DATA) cat(paste0(
  "::: {.callout-warning}\n",
  "This site was rendered from the bundled **synthetic sample data**, not ",
  "real economic data. Run `R/run_all.R` with free FRED and BLS API keys to ",
  "fetch the real series, then re-render.\n",
  ":::\n"))
```

```{=html}
<div class="hero">
  <p class="lead">A running snapshot of changes to the US economy through policy
  shifts starting in 2025 &mdash; labor, consumers, travel, public health, and
  markets, tracked on one continuously updated page.</p>
  <p class="hero-meta">Five live public data sources &middot; automated R
  pipeline &middot; updated weekly</p>
</div>
```

```{r}
#| output: asis
# Hero stats: the latest reading for each headline indicator, plus the change
# since a baseline the reader chooses. The change for every baseline is
# precomputed here and stored on each card as a data attribute; a small JS
# selector swaps which one is shown, so the page stays fully static.
baselines <- list(
  list(key = "oct2024", label = "October 2024",
       pick = function(d, v) mean(v[format(d, "%Y-%m") == "2024-10"], na.rm = TRUE)),
  list(key = "q42024", label = "the Q4 2024 average",
       pick = function(d, v) mean(v[format(d, "%Y-%m") %in%
                                    c("2024-10", "2024-11", "2024-12")], na.rm = TRUE)),
  list(key = "yearago", label = "one year earlier",
       pick = function(d, v) v[which.min(abs(d - (max(d) - 365)))]),
  list(key = "previous", label = "the previous reading",
       pick = function(d, v) if (length(v) > 1) v[length(v) - 1] else NA_real_)
)

kpi <- function(label, vals, dates, digits, suffix = "", show_pct = FALSE) {
  ok     <- !is.na(vals)
  d      <- as.Date(dates)[ok]; v <- vals[ok]
  o      <- order(d); d <- d[o]; v <- v[o]
  latest <- v[length(v)]
  fmt    <- function(x) formatC(x, format = "f", digits = digits, big.mark = ",")
  attrs  <- ""; default_txt <- ""
  for (b in baselines) {
    base <- suppressWarnings(b$pick(d, v))
    txt  <- if (!is.finite(base)) {
      sprintf("&mdash;<span class='kpi-sub'>no %s baseline</span>", b$label)
    } else {
      delta <- latest - base
      arrow <- if (delta > 0) "&#9650;" else if (delta < 0) "&#9660;" else "&middot;"
      # For level series (index, price), the percent change is the more
      # readable figure, so show both.
      pct <- if (show_pct && base != 0)
        sprintf(" (%s%.1f%%)", if (delta >= 0) "+" else "-",
                abs(delta / base * 100)) else ""
      sprintf("%s %s%s%s<span class='kpi-sub'>vs %s</span>",
              arrow, fmt(abs(delta)), suffix, pct, b$label)
    }
    attrs <- paste0(attrs, sprintf(' data-%s="%s"', b$key, txt))
    # Render the default baseline inline too, so the headline figure is there
    # before (and without) JavaScript; the selector just swaps it.
    if (b$key == "oct2024") default_txt <- gsub("'", '"', txt, fixed = TRUE)
  }
  sprintf(paste0(
    '<div class="kpi-card"%s><div class="kpi-label">%s</div>',
    '<div class="kpi-value">%s</div>',
    '<div class="kpi-current">Now %s%s',
    '<span class="kpi-asof">&middot; %s</span></div></div>'),
    attrs, label, default_txt, fmt(latest), suffix, format(max(d), "%b %Y"))
}

cat('<div class="kpi-controls"><label for="kpi-baseline">Compare with</label>',
    '<select id="kpi-baseline">',
    '<option value="oct2024" selected>October 2024 (pre-election)</option>',
    '<option value="q42024">Q4 2024 average</option>',
    '<option value="yearago">One year earlier</option>',
    '<option value="previous">Previous reading</option>',
    '</select></div>', sep = "")
cat('<div class="kpi-row">')
cat(kpi("Unemployment rate",   unrate_filtered$unrate_percent, unrate_filtered$date, 1, "%"))
cat(kpi("S&amp;P 500",         spy_dat$value,     spy_dat$date,      0, "",  show_pct = TRUE))
cat(kpi("Consumer prices (CPI)", cpi_dat$value,   cpi_dat$date,      1, "",  show_pct = TRUE))
cat(kpi("30-year mortgage",    mortgage_dat$value, mortgage_dat$date, 2, "%"))
cat('</div>')
```

Pick a category to explore the full series. Use the 1Y / 2Y / All buttons under
each chart to change the time window, hover any line for exact values, and note
the dashed grey line marking the November 2024 election.

```{=html}
<div class="category-grid">
  <a class="category-card" href="labor.html">
    <div class="cc-title">Labor</div>
    <div class="cc-desc">Unemployment, payrolls, jobless claims, wage growth, job openings</div>
  </a>
  <a class="category-card" href="consumers.html">
    <div class="cc-title">Consumers</div>
    <div class="cc-desc">Prices, gas, rent, mortgages, sentiment, vehicle sales, loans</div>
  </a>
  <a class="category-card" href="travel.html">
    <div class="cc-title">Travel</div>
    <div class="cc-desc">International arrivals, TSA volume, airline load factor</div>
  </a>
  <a class="category-card" href="health.html">
    <div class="cc-title">Public health</div>
    <div class="cc-desc">Confirmed measles cases by week of rash onset</div>
  </a>
  <a class="category-card" href="markets.html">
    <div class="cc-title">Markets</div>
    <div class="cc-desc">Treasury yields, the S&amp;P 500, the dollar, GDP nowcast, housing</div>
  </a>
  <a class="category-card" href="all.html">
    <div class="cc-title">All indicators</div>
    <div class="cc-desc">Every chart on one page, with the stacked / side-by-side toggle</div>
  </a>
</div>
```


{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
